### Fix 3
Line: 77
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @utils/components/DetailsItem/DetailsItem
  parentName: DetailsItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

```
   78                data-test="edit-annotations"

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/networking-console-plugin/source/src/views/routes/details/components/tabs/detailsTab/components/RouteDetailsSection/DetailsSectionLeftColumn.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 82): `Button.variant` type changed
The value `variant="link"` is still valid in the new type — `'link'` is present in both the old and new union types. The only change is the addition of `'stateful'`. **No code change needed.**

### Fix 2 (Line 9): `useAccessReview` type changed
Looking at the signature change for `useAccessReview`, the truncated signatures show the parameter types are the same (`resourceAttributes: AccessReviewResourceAttributes, impersonate?: Impersonat...`). The usage on line 39 passes an object with `group`, `name`, `namespace`, `resource`, `verb` — this is a standard `AccessReviewResourceAttributes` object. The current usage appears compatible. **No code change needed.**

### Fix 3 (Line 77): Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
Looking at lines 77-86, the `<Button>` contains both text content (line 84: the annotation count text) AND an icon (`<PencilAltIcon />`). Per the fix instructions:

> If `<Button>` contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
>   Before: `<Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>`
>   After:  `<Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>`

So I need to move `<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />` from children to the `icon` prop. Since PatternFly v6 handles icon styling automatically via the `icon` prop, the `className` on PencilAltIcon with the `pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain` class is no longer needed (it was a manual workaround). However, `co-icon-space-l` is a custom class for spacing. With the icon in the `icon` prop, PF handles the spacing, so I'll keep the icon clean.

arguments.after:
<Button
              data-test="edit-annotations"
              icon={<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />}
              isInline
              onClick={annotationsModalLauncher}
              type="button"
              variant="link"
            >
              {t('{{count}} annotation', { count: _.size(getAnnotations(route, {})) })}
            </Button>
arguments.before:
<Button
              data-test="edit-annotations"
              isInline
              onClick={annotationsModalLauncher}
              type="button"
              variant="link"
            >
              {t('{{count}} annotation', { count: _.size(getAnnotations(route, {})) })}
              <PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />
            </Button>

|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 (Line 82) | `Button.variant` type changed | **Already compatible — no change needed** | The value `variant="link"` is valid in both the old and new union types. The only change to the type was the *addition* of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing usage. |
| Fix 2 (Line 9) | `useAccessReview` constant type changed | **Already compatible — no change needed** | The `useAccessReview` signature change is internal/truncated and the current call-site usage (passing an `AccessReviewResourceAttributes` object) remains compatible. No code modification required. |
| Fix 3 (Line 77) | `sd-icon-children-to-prop-button` — icon children to `icon` prop | **Applied** | Moved `<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />` from Button's children to the `icon` prop (line 79). The Button now contains only the text child. The `PencilAltIcon` import is still used. |
